title: Refactor SRP-001 (SRP): The class combines multiple distinct responsibilities including input stream...

detected_issue.description: The class combines multiple distinct responsibilities including input stream management, JSON token traversal, various JSON value parsing (strings, numbers, booleans, null), JSON name parsing and canonicalization, Base64 decoding, character encoding/decoding, whitespace/comment skipping, and error reporting.
detected_issue.reasoning: This class is a 'God class' that handles too many unrelated concerns. For instance, `_loadMore()` (I/O), `nextToken()` (token traversal), `_parseUnsignedNumber()` (number parsing), `_parseName()` (name parsing/canonicalization), `_decodeBase64()` (Base64 decoding), and `_decodeEscaped()` (character decoding) are all part of this single class. A change in any of these areas (e.g., input buffering strategy, JSON number format rules, Base64 standard, character escaping rules) would require modifying this class, violating the 'one reason to change' principle.

fix_logic.summary: Extracts Base64 decoding logic into a dedicated UTF8Base64Decoder class.
fix_logic.rationale: The `UTF8StreamJsonParser` class previously handled Base64 decoding, which is a distinct responsibility unrelated to core JSON parsing or input stream management. By moving methods like `_decodeBase64`, `_readBinary`, and their associated helpers (`_decodeBase64Escape`, `_handleBase64MissingPadding`, `_reportInvalidBase64Char`) into a new `UTF8Base64Decoder` class, we adhere to the Single Responsibility Principle. The parser now delegates Base64 operations, reducing its complexity and making it easier to maintain or change Base64 decoding logic independently. The new `UTF8Base64Decoder` class interacts with the parser via a constructor reference to access necessary input state and error reporting mechanisms, minimizing changes to the parser's public API and internal state management.
